title: A top level parent module overrides a lower level child modules timeunit
body:
I have two modules top and bot (bot is instantiated under top).
When they have different timeunit definitions, the definition in top overrides bot.

(The ifdef will hide the problem for now)

```
module top ();
`ifdef DIFFERENT_TIMEUNIT
   timeunit 1ns;
`else
   timeunit 1s;
`endif
   timeprecision 1ps;

   logic 		 clkb, clk;

   initial
     begin
	clkb = 0;
     end

   always @(clk)
     begin
	clkb <= ~clk;
     end
   
   bot bot (.clkb(clkb), .clk(clk));
endmodule

module bot (input logic clkb, output logic clk);
   timeunit 1s;
   timeprecision 1fs;

   integer count;
   real    delay;
   
   initial
     begin
	count = 0;
	delay = 500e-9;
	clk = clkb;
	#(3.5 * delay) $display("Initial finishing at %g, clkb = %b",
			 $realtime(), clkb);
     end

   always @(clkb)
     begin
	$display("clkb is %b at %g", clkb, $realtime());
	count++;
	#(delay) clk = clkb;
     end

   always @(count)
     begin
	if (count > 20)
	  begin
	     $display("Finishing at time %g (%m)", $realtime());
	     $finish(1);
	 
	     if ($realtime() < (delay * 20))
	       begin
		  $display("%%Error: That was too quick! (%g)", $realtime());
	       end
	  end
     end

   final
     begin
	$display("Count was %0d at %g (%m)", count, $realtime());
     end
   
endmodule // bot

```

Compile and run the above:

```
verilator -exe -timing -cc -build top.sv -main --top-module top
./obj_dir/Vtop

```

With the ifdef hiding the problem, all is well.

```
clkb is 1 at 0
clkb is 0 at 5e-07
clkb is 1 at 1e-06
clkb is 0 at 1.5e-06
Initial finishing at 1.75e-06, clkb = 0
clkb is 1 at 2e-06
clkb is 0 at 2.5e-06
clkb is 1 at 3e-06
clkb is 0 at 3.5e-06
clkb is 1 at 4e-06
clkb is 0 at 4.5e-06
clkb is 1 at 5e-06
clkb is 0 at 5.5e-06
clkb is 1 at 6e-06
clkb is 0 at 6.5e-06
clkb is 1 at 7e-06
clkb is 0 at 7.5e-06
clkb is 1 at 8e-06
clkb is 0 at 8.5e-06
clkb is 1 at 9e-06
clkb is 0 at 9.5e-06
clkb is 1 at 1e-05
Finishing at time 1e-05 (TOP.top.bot)
- top.sv:52: Verilog $finish

```

Now we will activate the problem:

```
verilator -exe -timing -cc -build top.sv -main --top-module top +define+DIFFERENT_TIMEUNIT
./obj_dir/Vtop

```

```
clkb is 1 at 0
clkb is 0 at 1e-15
Initial finishing at 2e-15, clkb = 0
clkb is 1 at 2e-15
clkb is 0 at 3e-15
clkb is 1 at 4e-15
clkb is 0 at 5e-15
clkb is 1 at 6e-15
clkb is 0 at 7e-15
clkb is 1 at 8e-15
clkb is 0 at 9e-15
clkb is 1 at 1e-14
clkb is 0 at 1.1e-14
clkb is 1 at 1.2e-14
clkb is 0 at 1.3e-14
clkb is 1 at 1.4e-14
clkb is 0 at 1.5e-14
clkb is 1 at 1.6e-14
clkb is 0 at 1.7e-14
clkb is 1 at 1.8e-14
clkb is 0 at 1.9e-14
clkb is 1 at 2e-14
Finishing at time 2e-14 (TOP.top.bot)
- top.sv:52: Verilog $finish
%Error: That was too quick! (2e-14)

```

The timeunit in effect within bot should be influenced by the timeunits declared elsewhere.

It appears that only the # delays are affected?
